description: PAM faillock locks an account due to excessive password failures, this event must be logged.
rationale: Without auditing of these events it may be harder or impossible to identify what an attacker did after an attack.
ocil_clause: the "audit" option is not set, is missing or commented out
ocil:
Verify the "/etc/security/faillock.conf" file is configured to log user name information when unsuccessful logon attempts occur:

$ sudo grep audit /etc/security/faillock.conf

audit
fixtext:
Configure Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 to log user name information when unsuccessful logon attempts occur.

Add/Modify the "/etc/security/faillock.conf" file to match the following line:

audit
srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must log user name information when unsuccessful logon attempts occur.

stig.srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must log username information when unsuccessful logon attempts occur.
stig.vuldiscussion: Without auditing of these events, it may be harder or impossible to identify what an attacker did after an attack.
stig.checktext:
Verify the "/etc/security/faillock.conf" file is configured to log username information when unsuccessful logon attempts occur with the following command:

$ sudo grep audit /etc/security/faillock.conf

audit

If the "audit" option is not set, is missing, or is commented out, this is a finding.
stig.fixtext:
Configure Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 to log username information when unsuccessful logon attempts occur.

Enable the feature using the following command:

$ sudo authselect enable-feature with-faillock

Add/modify the "/etc/security/faillock.conf" file to match the following line:

audit

title: Account Lockouts Must Be Logged
